title: Open IR interpretation rules for the supported MLIR, LLVM, and CIRCT surface

metadata.content_policy: Project-authored short paraphrases and citations only; referenced specifications and repositories are not redistributed.
metadata.coverage_boundary: Current MLIR/LLVM text parsers preserve structural evidence; no executable language-spec compatibility binding is published.
metadata.truth_boundary: Default text parsing is not a language-spec attestation. OpenIR rules remain lexical guidance until a capability-authorized, persisted semantic-adapter contract exists.
metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Complete-domain aggregation and masks, artifact-revision attestation, ambiguity handling, stable evidence identity, and prohibitions on promoting IR containment, CFG, calls, SSA uses, or dialect operations directly to hardware topology are HLSGraph policies. The pinned sources establish only the native IR syntax and semantics named by each rule.

documents.title: MLIR Language Reference
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the documentation is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: MLIR Builtin Location Attribute Definitions
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the documentation is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: LLVM Language Reference Manual
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the documentation is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: Source Level Debugging with LLVM
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the documentation is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: CIRCT Handshake Operation Definitions
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the documentation is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: ScaleHLS: A New Scalable High-Level Synthesis Framework on Multi-Level Intermediate Representation
documents.license_note: Citation metadata only; the paper and repository are not redistributed.

documents.title: Dynamatic MLIR Primer
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the documentation and repository are not included in HLSGraph.

rules.title: Preserve MLIR module and function scope as IR evidence
rules.summary: MLIR operations are nested in regions and blocks, while module and function operations provide compiler IR containment and scope.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Compiler IR containment is preserved as scope evidence; it does not by itself establish an instantiated HLS hardware block.

rules.title: Preserve dialect identity and pass stage
rules.summary: MLIR supports multiple dialects, and an operation's semantics are defined by the dialect that owns that operation.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: HLSGraph preserves dialect identity and does not promote a generic operation to hardware topology without a supported dialect-specific projection.

rules.title: Do not assign one topology meaning to every MLIR region
rules.summary: MLIR regions contain blocks, and the operation that owns a region defines the region's semantics.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Region containment or SSA use alone is not promoted to HLS connectivity; the owning operation's supported semantics are required.

rules.title: Use typed MLIR Location as mapping evidence
rules.summary: MLIR location attributes represent source locations and support structured forms including call-site, fused, named, and opaque locations.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Allowing explicit many-to-many mappings, retaining ambiguity, and forbidding name-based edge invention are HLSGraph mapping policies; the source defines the typed location forms.

rules.title: Require dialect-qualified operation aggregates
rules.summary: MLIR operations have dialect-qualified names and may carry operands, results, attributes, regions, and successors.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Operation aggregates use dialect-qualified names and require an explicitly complete current-artifact domain; missing evidence remains unknown rather than zero.

rules.title: Treat LLVM modules and functions as IR scope
rules.summary: LLVM function definitions contain typed signatures and a list of basic blocks that together form the function CFG.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: LLVM function containment is retained as low-level IR scope evidence and is not treated as proof of an instantiated HLS hardware block.

rules.title: Preserve LLVM instructions as typed operation evidence
rules.summary: LLVM instruction syntax is opcode-specific and includes explicit types for values where the instruction requires them.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: An LLVM instruction is compiler-operation evidence and is not treated as a scheduled or function-unit-bound hardware operation without schedule or binding evidence.

rules.title: Keep LLVM CFG out of HLS topology
rules.summary: An LLVM function definition contains basic blocks that end in terminator instructions and together form the function CFG.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: LLVM basic blocks and successor relations remain low-level control evidence and are not promoted directly to HLS hardware edges.

rules.title: Keep resolved LLVM calls out of hardware topology
rules.summary: The LLVM call instruction records a callee, function type, arguments, optional attributes, and result semantics.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: A resolved LLVM call-like instruction remains low-level compiler evidence and does not by itself establish an HLS hardware instance or topology edge.

rules.title: Preserve LLVM debug locations without claiming uniqueness
rules.summary: LLVM debug metadata records source-level information and may be updated or dropped as IR transformations change the program.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Debug locations are retained as potentially incomplete, many-to-many mapping evidence and never treated as unique hardware identity.

rules.title: Require opcode-qualified LLVM operation aggregates
rules.summary: The LLVM instruction reference groups the instruction set into named classes and defines opcode-specific instruction syntax.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: LLVM operation histograms are recomputed from explicit opcodes over a declared complete current-artifact domain.

rules.title: Require explicit LLVM GEP index operand aggregates
rules.summary: The getelementptr instruction syntax explicitly lists a base pointer followed by zero or more typed index operands.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Requiring a complete aggregation domain and treating absent evidence as unknown rather than zero are HLSGraph derivation policies.

rules.title: Require explicit LLVM integer width aggregates
rules.summary: LLVM integer types use the form iN, where N is the integer type's bit width.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Bitwidth aggregates count explicit integer type occurrences in a complete current-artifact domain and do not invent absent widths.

rules.title: Require typed LLVM memory-access aggregates
rules.summary: LLVM defines distinct memory and addressing opcodes including alloca, load, store, fence, cmpxchg, atomicrmw, and getelementptr.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Memory-access aggregates are recomputed from explicit LLVM opcodes and do not identify eventual HLS banks or ports without separate binding evidence.

rules.title: Retain native Handshake dataflow-region evidence
rules.summary: The CIRCT Handshake dialect defines fine-grained dataflow operations together with a Handshake function operation.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no source text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Keeping native Handshake evidence separate from post-synthesis topology and leaving unsupported operations evidence-only are HLSGraph truth-boundary policies.

entries.section: Functions, Instructions, Types, Control Flow, Calls, and Memory Access

entries.rationale: The publication describes ScaleHLS but does not define HLSGraph's adapter or semantic-attestation contract; no executable public rule is inferred.
The project documentation provides adapter context but does not define HLSGraph's semantic-attestation contract; no executable public rule is inferred.
